summary: The R1 concern that the hard-to-vary and validation arguments lacked a claim-test map has been addressed. The revised evaluation includes a claim-evidence map, and the review artifact summarizes the same evidence boundaries.
strengths: The main claim is limited to human-invariant-only detectors under tested A2A scenarios.
The fixed-threshold and re-optimized-threshold recall figures are reported separately.
The per-address framework's 0% recall on Chain 7 is framed as an unresolved architectural boundary.

theorem_assumptions_checked: The detector family relies only on human behavioral invariants
The evaluated scenarios are the A2A cases described in the manuscript
Agent-native signals are not included in the baseline detector family
proof_audits.notes: The proposition is scoped to human-invariant-only detectors and the tested A2A scenarios. The revised manuscript no longer asserts a universal detector impossibility theorem.
